articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Federal Fiscal Autonomy in Landmark Ruling on GST Compensation
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today delivered a pivotal judgment, reinforcing the principles of fiscal federalism by ruling on the modalities of Goods and Services Tax (GST) compensation to states.
• The ruling clarifies the Centre's obligations and states' rights regarding revenue shortfalls, particularly in the post-GST compensation period.
• It emphasizes cooperative federalism while safeguarding the financial independence of state governments, drawing attention to Article 280 and the Finance Commission's role.
• Legal experts suggest the judgment will have long-term implications for Centre-State financial relations and future tax reforms, potentially influencing the 16th Finance Commission's recommendations.
• The verdict comes amidst ongoing debates about the distribution of financial resources and states' fiscal space, providing much-needed clarity.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution - Federalism
Centre-State Financial Relations
GST Council
Fiscal Policy
Judicial Review
Finance Commission
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court's role in federal disputes
Concepts of GST compensation and its constitutional basis
Constitutional provisions related to Centre-State financial relations (e.g., Articles 268-281)
Role and mandate of the Finance Commission
Meaning of fiscal autonomy and its importance in federal structure.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's judgment on fiscal federalism in India, discussing its impact on the distribution of financial resources.
Discuss how the ruling balances the Centre's financial obligations with states' fiscal autonomy, particularly in the context of revenue sharing and tax devolution.
Evaluate the potential impact on future GST Council deliberations and inter-state economic disparities, considering the shift in compensation mechanisms.
Examine the role of the judiciary in resolving Centre-State disputes and upholding constitutional principles related to federal structure and financial governance.
articles.imageDescription: An illustration depicting the Supreme Court of India alongside a map of India highlighting Centre-State financial flows and the GST logo.
articles.trivia: The concept of GST compensation was initially for five years, ending in June 2022, but states have continuously sought extensions or alternative mechanisms due to revenue uncertainties.

articles.headline: Veteran Communist Leader V. S. Achuthanandan Passes Away at 101
articles.explainer:
• V. S. Achuthanandan, a towering figure in Kerala politics and former Chief Minister, passed away at the age of 101.
• Known for his unwavering commitment to socialist ideals and as a fierce advocate for the marginalized, he served as Chief Minister of Kerala from 2006 to 2011.
• Achuthanandan was a prominent leader of the Communist Party of India (Marxist) and a seven-time Member of the Legislative Assembly (MLA) from various constituencies in Kerala.
• His political career spanned over eight decades, making him one of India's longest-serving politicians.
• He was celebrated for his anti-corruption stances and his role in various social movements and land reforms in Kerala.
gsSubTopics: Post-Independence Consolidation and Reorganization within India
Political Philosophies
Important Personalities
Kerala Politics
prelimsPoints: V. S. Achuthanandan's role as CM of Kerala
His association with CPI(M)
Significance of Kerala's land reforms
General knowledge about prominent political figures.
mainsPoints: Discuss the contributions of V. S. Achuthanandan to Kerala's political and social landscape, particularly in the context of land reforms and social justice.
Analyze the evolution of communist politics in Kerala, using Achuthanandan's career as a case study.
Evaluate the impact of long-serving political figures on state governance and policy-making in India.
Examine the 'Kerala Model' of development and the role played by leaders like Achuthanandan in its shaping.
articles.imageDescription: A black and white photograph of V. S. Achuthanandan from his younger days, alongside a more recent portrait.
articles.trivia: V. S. Achuthanandan was the last surviving member of the 32 communist leaders who walked out of the CPI National Council in 1964 to form the CPI(M).

articles.headline: India-EU FTA Negotiations Progress Towards Key Chapters
articles.explainer:
• Talks for the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) showed significant progress today, with negotiators reportedly nearing consensus on crucial chapters.
• Key areas of discussion included market access for goods, services, investment protection, and intellectual property rights.
• Both sides expressed optimism about bridging remaining gaps, aiming for a comprehensive and mutually beneficial agreement.
• An FTA with the EU, India's third-largest trading partner, is expected to boost bilateral trade and investment significantly.
• The agreement is seen as a strategic move to diversify supply chains and strengthen economic ties amidst global geopolitical shifts.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, Regional and Global Groupings and Agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of Policies and Politics of Developed and Developing Countries on India’s interests
Indian Economy and issues relating to Planning, Mobilization of Resources, Growth, Development and Employment
prelimsPoints: Current status of India-EU relations
Key objectives of FTA negotiations
Major trading partners of India
Concept of trade blocs and economic integration.
Difference between FTA, CEPA, and Customs Union.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of a comprehensive India-EU Free Trade Agreement for India's foreign policy and economic growth.
Discuss the potential challenges and opportunities for various sectors of the Indian economy arising from the FTA.
Evaluate how this agreement aligns with India's broader trade policy objectives and its role in global supply chain diversification.
Examine the role of FTAs in shaping India's international relations and its position in the multilateral trading system.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union superimposed over a graph showing upward trade trends.
articles.trivia: Negotiations for an India-EU FTA were initially launched in 2007 but stalled in 2013 before being officially re-launched in 2022.

articles.headline: Government Unveils National AI Strategy 2.0 Focusing on Ethical Development and Inclusive Growth
articles.explainer:
• The Indian government today unveiled its National AI Strategy 2.0, emphasizing ethical considerations, data privacy, and inclusive access to Artificial Intelligence.
• The updated strategy outlines a roadmap for leveraging AI across critical sectors like healthcare, agriculture, education, and governance.
• Key pillars include fostering AI research and development, building a skilled workforce, and establishing robust regulatory frameworks for responsible AI deployment.
• Focus is placed on developing 'AI for India' initiatives, addressing local challenges and promoting indigenous AI solutions.
• The strategy aims to position India as a global leader in ethical and sustainable AI development, aligning with its digital public infrastructure goals.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- Developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in Science & Technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, Robotics, Nano-technology, Bio-technology and issues relating to Intellectual Property Rights
Government Policies and Interventions
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: Objectives of National AI Strategy 2.0
Key sectors identified for AI application in India
Government initiatives related to AI (e.g., INDIAai)
Concepts of ethical AI and data governance
Role of MeitY in technology policy.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the key features and objectives of India's National AI Strategy 2.0, particularly its emphasis on ethical development and inclusive growth.
Discuss the potential opportunities and challenges for India in becoming a global leader in AI, considering its demographic dividend and digital infrastructure.
Evaluate the role of AI in transforming sectors like healthcare, agriculture, and education in India, citing specific examples.
Examine the regulatory and governance frameworks necessary to ensure responsible and ethical deployment of AI technologies in a diverse society like India.
articles.imageDescription: A futuristic graphic depicting interconnected networks and data flowing into a stylized brain icon, symbolizing AI.
articles.trivia: India was one of the first countries to publish a national strategy for Artificial Intelligence in 2018, titled 'National Strategy for Artificial Intelligence: #AIforAll'.

articles.headline: ISRO's Chandrayaan-4 Rover Completes Lunar South Pole Exploration Mission
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today announced the successful completion of the Chandrayaan-4 rover's mission at the lunar South Pole.
• The rover, named 'Pragyan-2', has transmitted invaluable data and high-resolution images, significantly enhancing our understanding of lunar geology and potential water ice reserves.
• The mission confirmed the presence of various elements and provided detailed topographical maps of previously unexplored regions.
• This achievement marks another milestone for India's lunar exploration program, building upon the successes of Chandrayaan-2 and Chandrayaan-3.
• Scientists are now analyzing the vast dataset, which is expected to yield new insights into the Moon's formation and evolution.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- Developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in Science & Technology
Space Technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: Objectives of Chandrayaan-4 mission
Key findings regarding lunar South Pole
Names of ISRO's lunar missions (Chandrayaan-1, 2, 3, 4)
Significance of water ice on Moon
Role of ISRO in global space exploration.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of ISRO's Chandrayaan-4 mission in advancing humanity's understanding of the Moon, particularly the South Pole region.
Evaluate India's growing capabilities in space technology and its contributions to global space exploration, citing examples of recent missions.
Examine the potential scientific and economic implications of discoveries related to lunar water ice and resources.
Analyze how India's space program contributes to national development and scientific temper.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendition of the Chandrayaan-4 rover 'Pragyan-2' exploring the rugged terrain of the lunar South Pole.
articles.trivia: India became the fourth country to successfully land a spacecraft on the Moon with Chandrayaan-3 in 2023, and the first to land near the lunar South Pole.

articles.headline: India to Implement New Coastal Zone Management Plan for Sustainable Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change today announced the implementation of a comprehensive new Coastal Zone Management Plan (CZMP).
• The plan focuses on balancing economic development with ecological conservation along India's vast coastline, incorporating climate change resilience measures.
• It introduces stricter regulations for construction, industrial activities, and tourism in ecologically sensitive areas, while promoting sustainable livelihoods for coastal communities.
• The CZMP integrates geospatial technology for better planning and enforcement, aiming for a more scientific approach to coastal governance.
• Public consultations were a key part of its formulation, ensuring local community participation in protection and development efforts.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, Environmental Pollution and Degradation, Environmental Impact Assessment
Coastal Regulation Zone (CRZ) Notification
Government Policies and Interventions
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Key features of the new CZMP
Purpose of Coastal Regulation Zone (CRZ) notifications
Role of Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
Concept of Blue Economy
Importance of coastal ecosystems (e.g., mangroves, coral reefs).
mainsPoints: Discuss the need for an updated Coastal Zone Management Plan in India, considering the challenges of climate change, population pressure, and economic development.
Analyze the key provisions of the new CZMP and evaluate its potential effectiveness in achieving sustainable coastal development and conservation.
Examine the role of technology and community participation in strengthening coastal governance and mitigating environmental degradation.
Critically assess the balance struck between economic activities and ecological protection in the new plan, considering the livelihoods of coastal communities.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of a vibrant coastal area showing both natural ecosystems (mangroves) and regulated human settlements.
articles.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 km, supporting diverse ecosystems and livelihoods, making coastal zone management crucial.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Persistent Inflationary Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5%, citing persistent inflationary pressures.
• This decision aligns with market expectations, as the central bank prioritizes price stability while supporting economic growth.
• The MPC noted that while core inflation has shown signs of easing, food inflation remains elevated and global uncertainties pose risks.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to closely monitor evolving economic indicators and stand ready to take appropriate policy actions.
• Experts suggest the 'wait and watch' approach reflects a cautious stance, aiming to anchor inflation expectations without stifling growth.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to Planning, Mobilization of Resources, Growth, Development and Employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
Inflation
prelimsPoints: Current repo rate and its implications
Functions of the RBI and MPC
Definition of inflation and its types (e.g., core, food)
Tools of monetary policy (e.g., repo, CRR, SLR)
Relationship between interest rates, inflation, and economic growth.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the status quo on the repo rate, considering the current economic landscape and inflationary trends.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in managing inflation and fostering economic growth in India's dynamic economic environment.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing price stability with supporting growth, particularly in the context of global economic uncertainties and supply-side shocks.
Examine the coordination between fiscal and monetary policies in achieving macroeconomic stability in India.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting the RBI logo with upward and downward arrows, symbolizing interest rate decisions and inflation control.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) comprises six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: Noted Meteorologist and Climate Scientist Sulochana Gadgil Dies at 81
articles.explainer:
• Dr. Sulochana Gadgil, an internationally renowned meteorologist and climate scientist, passed away at the age of 81.
• She was widely recognized for her pioneering research on the Indian monsoon system, its variability, and its impact on agriculture.
• Dr. Gadgil's work significantly contributed to understanding monsoon dynamics, aiding in better climate prediction models.
• She was a recipient of numerous accolades, including the Padma Bhushan, for her profound contributions to atmospheric science.
• Her legacy includes mentoring generations of scientists and advocating for interdisciplinary research in climate science.
gsSubTopics: Important Personalities
Geographical phenomena
Climate Change
Indian Meteorology
prelimsPoints: Contributions of Sulochana Gadgil to Indian meteorology
Key aspects of the Indian Monsoon (e.g., factors influencing it)
Significance of monsoon for Indian agriculture
Organizations involved in meteorological research in India (e.g., IMD, IITM).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Dr. Sulochana Gadgil's research on the Indian monsoon and its impact on agriculture and climate science.
Analyze the challenges and complexities of monsoon prediction in India and how scientific advancements have improved forecasting.
Evaluate the broader implications of climate variability on India's economy and society, particularly for the agricultural sector.
Examine the role of eminent scientists in shaping national research priorities and fostering scientific temper.
articles.imageDescription: A portrait of Dr. Sulochana Gadgil, with a subtle background image of monsoon clouds over India.
articles.trivia: Sulochana Gadgil was the first Indian woman to be elected as a Fellow of the American Meteorological Society.

articles.headline: National Cyber Security Policy 2025 Emphasizes Public-Private Partnership and Skill Development
articles.explainer:
• The Indian government today unveiled the National Cyber Security Policy 2025, a comprehensive framework aimed at strengthening the nation's digital defenses.
• The policy prioritizes public-private partnerships, encouraging collaboration between government agencies, private sector, and academia to build a resilient cyber ecosystem.
• Key focus areas include enhancing critical information infrastructure protection, promoting cyber hygiene, and developing a skilled cybersecurity workforce.
• It also addresses international cooperation on cyber issues and establishes a robust incident response mechanism.
• The policy seeks to create a secure and trusted digital environment essential for India's digital economy and national security.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Cyber warfare
Role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges
Internal security challenges through communication networks
Government Policies and Interventions
prelimsPoints: Key objectives of National Cyber Security Policy 2025
Concept of critical information infrastructure
Role of various government bodies in cyber security (e.g., CERT-In, NCCC)
Importance of public-private partnership in cyber defense
Threats posed by cyber warfare and cybercrime.
mainsPoints: Analyze the critical components of India's National Cyber Security Policy 2025 and its potential impact on safeguarding national digital assets.
Discuss the significance of public-private partnerships in building a robust cybersecurity framework, citing examples of successful collaborations.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing a comprehensive cyber security policy in a rapidly digitizing economy like India, including skill gaps and evolving threats.
Examine the role of international cooperation in combating cross-border cyber threats and ensuring a secure global digital space.
articles.imageDescription: A digital graphic showing a shield protecting a network of interconnected devices, symbolizing cybersecurity.
articles.trivia: India's first National Cyber Security Policy was launched in 2013, focusing on protecting information infrastructure and promoting secure cyberspace.

articles.headline: Ratan Thiyam, Iconic Playwright and Theatre Director, Passes Away at 77
articles.explainer:
• Ratan Thiyam, the legendary Indian playwright and theatre director, passed away at the age of 77, leaving an indelible mark on contemporary Indian theatre.
• Hailing from Manipur, Thiyam was celebrated for his unique theatrical style that blended traditional Manipuri forms with modern global aesthetics.
• His works often explored themes of conflict, human suffering, and cultural identity, resonating deeply with audiences worldwide.
• He was the founder-director of the Chorus Repertory Theatre and a recipient of the Padma Shri and Sangeet Natak Akademi Award.
• Thiyam's innovative use of music, movement, and visual artistry transformed Indian theatre and brought it international acclaim.
gsSubTopics: Indian Culture - Salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Important Personalities
prelimsPoints: Contributions of Ratan Thiyam to Indian theatre
Key characteristics of Manipuri theatre/dance forms
Major awards in Indian performing arts (e.g., Sangeet Natak Akademi Award)
Notable theatre personalities in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the unique contributions of Ratan Thiyam to contemporary Indian theatre, highlighting how he blended traditional forms with modern aesthetics.
Analyze how theatre, as an art form, can be used to address social and political issues, drawing examples from Thiyam's works.
Examine the role of regional artists and cultural institutions in promoting and preserving India's diverse artistic heritage.
Evaluate the challenges faced by performing arts in India today and suggest measures for their promotion and sustenance.
articles.imageDescription: A black and white photograph of Ratan Thiyam, perhaps during a theatre rehearsal, reflecting his intense artistic vision.
articles.trivia: Ratan Thiyam's production of 'Chakravyuh' (The Wheel of War) is considered one of his most iconic works and has been performed globally.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Bharat Krishi Samriddhi Yojana' to Boost Agri-Exports and Farmer Income
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government today launched the 'Bharat Krishi Samriddhi Yojana' (BKSY), a new scheme aimed at significantly boosting India's agricultural exports.
• The scheme focuses on creating robust supply chains, improving post-harvest infrastructure, and promoting value-added agricultural products.
• It offers financial incentives and technical assistance to farmers and FPOs (Farmer Producer Organizations) for adopting export-oriented farming practices.
• BKSY seeks to diversify India's export basket, targeting new international markets for high-value agricultural commodities.
• The initiative aligns with the government's vision of doubling farmer incomes and establishing India as a global agricultural powerhouse.
gsSubTopics: Major Crops - Cropping Patterns in various parts of the country
Different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage
Transport and Marketing of Agricultural Produce and issues and related constraints
E-technology in the aid of farmers
Food Processing and related industries in India- scope and significance
Government Policies and Interventions
prelimsPoints: Objectives of Bharat Krishi Samriddhi Yojana
Role of FPOs in agriculture
Government initiatives for farmer welfare and agri-exports
Key challenges in Indian agricultural exports
Concept of value-added agriculture.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives and potential impact of the 'Bharat Krishi Samriddhi Yojana' on India's agricultural sector and farmer incomes.
Discuss the challenges faced by Indian agri-exports and how the new scheme aims to address them through infrastructure development and market access.
Evaluate the role of Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs) and value addition in transforming the agricultural supply chain and promoting export competitiveness.
Examine how this scheme contributes to the broader goal of making India a global agricultural powerhouse and ensuring food security.
articles.imageDescription: A vibrant image of farmers working in a field with a backdrop of modern agricultural machinery and export containers.
articles.trivia: India is one of the world's largest producers of several agricultural commodities, yet its share in global agri-exports remains relatively low.

articles.headline: India Hosts International Conference on Renewable Energy Integration and Grid Modernization
articles.explainer:
• India today hosted a major International Conference on Renewable Energy Integration and Grid Modernization, bringing together global experts and policymakers.
• The conference focused on strategies to seamlessly integrate a higher share of renewable energy into national grids, addressing technical and regulatory challenges.
• Discussions included advancements in energy storage solutions, smart grid technologies, and cross-border energy trade.
• India showcased its progress in renewable energy deployment and its ambitious targets for achieving net-zero emissions.
• The event underscored India's leadership in promoting sustainable energy transition and fostering international collaboration.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, Environmental Pollution and Degradation, Environmental Impact Assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- Developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
International Relations
prelimsPoints: India's renewable energy targets (e.g., 500 GW by 2030)
Concepts of grid integration and smart grids
Different types of energy storage technologies
Role of international collaborations in climate action (e.g., ISA)
Challenges of intermittency in renewable energy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of renewable energy integration for India's energy security and climate change mitigation goals.
Analyze the technical, economic, and regulatory challenges involved in modernizing India's grid to accommodate a higher share of renewable energy.
Evaluate the role of international cooperation and technology transfer in accelerating India's sustainable energy transition.
Examine the potential of energy storage solutions and smart grid technologies in addressing the intermittency of renewable sources and ensuring grid stability.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting solar panels and wind turbines connected to a modern energy grid, with a globe in the background.
articles.trivia: India's installed renewable energy capacity (excluding large hydro) has grown significantly in recent years, making it one of the top five countries globally.

articles.headline: New Guidelines for Online Gaming Industry to Ensure Consumer Protection and Responsible Gaming
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) today released new comprehensive guidelines for the online gaming industry in India.
• The guidelines aim to ensure consumer protection, prevent addiction, and promote responsible gaming practices, particularly for minors.
• Key provisions include mandatory KYC verification, self-regulatory mechanisms, and transparent disclosure of game odds and financial risks.
• The framework also addresses issues of data privacy, grievance redressal, and the prohibition of games involving betting or gambling where prohibited by law.
• This move is expected to bring greater regulatory clarity and foster a safe and sustainable growth environment for the burgeoning online gaming sector.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Welfare Schemes for Vulnerable Sections of the population by the Centre and States and the Performance of these Schemes
Role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Cyber Security
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: Role of MeitY in regulating digital services
Importance of consumer protection in digital economy
Concept of KYC verification
Challenges of online gaming (e.g., addiction, financial fraud)
Regulatory framework for emerging digital sectors.
mainsPoints: Discuss the need for new guidelines for the online gaming industry in India, considering its rapid growth and associated social and economic challenges.
Analyze the key provisions of the new guidelines and evaluate their potential effectiveness in ensuring consumer protection and promoting responsible gaming.
Examine the role of self-regulatory bodies and technology in fostering a safe and ethical online gaming environment, particularly for vulnerable populations.
Critically assess the balance between regulating the online gaming sector and allowing for innovation and economic growth within the digital economy.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized graphic showing a mobile phone screen displaying a game, with a shield icon overlayed, representing protection.
articles.trivia: India's online gaming market is one of the fastest-growing globally, projected to reach multi-billion-dollar valuations in the coming years.

articles.headline: Report Highlights Progress in Jal Jeevan Mission, Focus on Quality Standards
articles.explainer:
• A recent report released by the Ministry of Jal Shakti highlights significant progress in the implementation of the Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM) across rural India.
• The mission has achieved a major milestone in providing tap water connections to a large percentage of rural households since its inception.
• The report specifically emphasizes the enhanced focus on ensuring the quality of water supplied, with increased testing and community participation in monitoring.
• Challenges related to source sustainability and maintenance of infrastructure in remote areas were also acknowledged, with plans for further interventions.
• JJM aims to provide safe and adequate drinking water through individual household tap connections by 2024, a key social welfare initiative.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Welfare Schemes for Vulnerable Sections of the population by the Centre and States and the Performance of these Schemes
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Water Management
prelimsPoints: Objectives and targets of Jal Jeevan Mission
Ministry responsible for JJM
Concept of 'Har Ghar Jal'
Importance of water quality testing
Challenges in rural water supply.
mainsPoints: Discuss the progress and impact of the Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM) in transforming rural water supply in India, highlighting its achievements and challenges.
Analyze the importance of focusing on water quality standards and source sustainability for the long-term success of such a large-scale welfare scheme.
Evaluate the role of community participation and local governance in the effective implementation and maintenance of rural water infrastructure.
Examine how access to safe and adequate drinking water contributes to public health, women's empowerment, and overall rural development.
articles.imageDescription: A visual showing a rural Indian woman happily filling water from a tap connection, symbolizing improved access.
articles.trivia: Jal Jeevan Mission was launched in August 2019 with the vision to provide safe and adequate drinking water to every rural household in India by 2024.

articles.headline: India and Japan Strengthen Strategic Partnership with New Defence Cooperation Initiatives
articles.explainer:
• India and Japan today announced new initiatives to bolster their strategic partnership, primarily focusing on enhanced defence cooperation and security collaboration.
• The discussions, held at a high-level bilateral meeting, included joint military exercises, technology transfer, and greater interoperability between their armed forces.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, emphasizing maritime security and regional stability.
• The enhanced partnership also explores cooperation in emerging technologies and critical infrastructure protection.
• This move signifies a deepening alignment between two key regional powers, aiming to counter regional security challenges and promote a rules-based international order.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, Regional and Global Groupings and Agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of Policies and Politics of Developed and Developing Countries on India’s interests
Security challenges and their management in border areas
Defence Technology
prelimsPoints: Key aspects of India-Japan strategic partnership
Importance of Indo-Pacific region
Major joint military exercises between India and Japan
Concept of a 'rules-based international order'
Significance of defense technology cooperation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the growing strategic convergence between India and Japan, particularly in the context of defence cooperation and their shared vision for the Indo-Pacific.
Discuss the implications of enhanced India-Japan security ties for regional stability and the balance of power in Asia.
Evaluate the potential for technology transfer and joint development in defence between the two nations, considering India's 'Make in India' initiative.
Examine how this bilateral partnership contributes to multilateral initiatives like the Quad and reinforces a rules-based international order.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan side-by-side, with a naval vessel in the background, symbolizing maritime and defense cooperation.
articles.trivia: India and Japan are both members of the Quadrilateral Security Dialogue (Quad), along with the United States and Australia.

articles.headline: M. K. Muthu, Actor and Politician, Passes Away at 77
articles.explainer:
• M. K. Muthu, the veteran Tamil actor and politician, passed away at the age of 77.
• Son of former Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M. Karunanidhi, Muthu had a brief but notable career in Tamil cinema during the 1970s.
• He also ventured into politics, following in his father's footsteps, though his political journey was less prominent.
• His passing marks the end of an era for a family deeply entrenched in Tamil Nadu's political and cultural landscape.
• Muthu was known for his melodious voice and acting prowess in films like 'Pillaiyo Pillai' and 'Namma Kuzhandaigal'.
gsSubTopics: Indian Culture - Salient aspects of Art Forms
Important Personalities
Regional Politics
prelimsPoints: Brief facts about M.K. Muthu's career
Association with M. Karunanidhi
General knowledge about prominent figures in regional cinema and politics.
articles.imageDescription: A vintage photograph of M. K. Muthu from his acting days.
articles.trivia: M. K. Muthu was the first son of M. Karunanidhi, a five-time Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu.

articles.headline: Chandra Barot, Director of Iconic Film 'Don', Passes Away at 86
articles.explainer:
• Chandra Barot, the acclaimed film director best known for the 1978 Bollywood classic 'Don', passed away at the age of 86.
• His directorial debut 'Don', starring Amitabh Bachchan, became a blockbuster and cult classic, influencing generations of filmmakers.
• Barot was also known for his work as an assistant director to renowned filmmakers like Manoj Kumar.
• His demise marks the loss of a significant voice from the golden era of Hindi cinema.
• The original 'Don' was celebrated for its gripping screenplay, memorable dialogues, and iconic music.
gsSubTopics: Indian Culture - Salient aspects of Art Forms
Important Personalities
Film Industry
prelimsPoints: Chandra Barot's most famous film 'Don'
Key figures in Hindi cinema history
General knowledge about iconic Indian films.
articles.imageDescription: A still from the movie 'Don' featuring Amitabh Bachchan, with Chandra Barot's name highlighted.
articles.trivia: The 1978 film 'Don' was remade in 2006 with Shah Rukh Khan in the lead, also becoming a commercial success.

articles.headline: Dr. P. Namperumalsamy, Visionary Ophthalmologist, Passes Away at 85
articles.explainer:
• Dr. P. Namperumalsamy, a globally acclaimed ophthalmologist and pioneer in community eye care, passed away at 85.
• He was the founder of Aravind Eye Care System, which revolutionized eye care delivery in India and globally through its high-volume, high-quality, and affordable model.
• His 'camps-based' approach significantly reduced preventable blindness, especially among the rural poor.
• Dr. Namperumalsamy was a recipient of the Padma Bhushan and the Public Service Award from the American Academy of Ophthalmology.
• His work transformed the landscape of public health, making quality eye care accessible to millions.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Welfare Schemes for Vulnerable Sections
Important Personalities
prelimsPoints: Contributions of Dr. P. Namperumalsamy to ophthalmology and public health
Aravind Eye Care System model
Concept of 'preventable blindness'
Government initiatives for eye care in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the innovative model of the Aravind Eye Care System as pioneered by Dr. P. Namperumalsamy and its impact on public health in India.
Analyze how a high-volume, high-quality, and affordable healthcare delivery system can address the challenges of accessibility and equity in developing countries.
Evaluate the role of social entrepreneurship in solving critical public health issues like preventable blindness.
Examine the lessons that can be drawn from the Aravind model for other healthcare sectors in India.
articles.imageDescription: A photograph of Dr. P. Namperumalsamy, smiling, with a subtle background of people receiving eye care.
articles.trivia: Aravind Eye Care System performs over 5 million outpatient visits and 500,000 surgeries annually, making it one of the largest eye care providers globally.

articles.headline: C. V. Padmarajan, Former Kerala Minister and Veteran Politician, Passes Away at 93
articles.explainer:
• C. V. Padmarajan, a seasoned politician and former minister in Kerala, passed away at the age of 93.
• He was a prominent leader of the Indian National Congress in Kerala, known for his administrative acumen and parliamentary skills.
• Padmarajan held various ministerial portfolios during his career, contributing to several key policy decisions in the state.
• His political journey spanned several decades, witnessing significant transformations in Kerala's political landscape.
• He was respected across party lines for his integrity and dedication to public service.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution - Historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Parliament and State Legislatures - structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Important Personalities
prelimsPoints: C. V. Padmarajan's association with Kerala politics and Congress
General knowledge about former state ministers
Role of state legislatures.
articles.imageDescription: A formal portrait of C. V. Padmarajan.
articles.trivia: Kerala has a history of coalition governments, with both the Congress-led UDF and CPI(M)-led LDF alternating in power.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: State Legislature and Council of Ministers

highlights: Supreme Court Upholds Federal Fiscal Autonomy in Landmark Ruling on GST Compensation, clarifying Centre-State financial relations.
Veteran Communist Leader V. S. Achuthanandan, former Kerala Chief Minister and a towering figure in state politics, passes away at 101.
India-EU Free Trade Agreement negotiations show significant progress, nearing consensus on crucial chapters to boost bilateral trade.
Government Unveils National AI Strategy 2.0, focusing on ethical development, data privacy, and inclusive growth across key sectors.
ISRO's Chandrayaan-4 rover successfully completes its lunar South Pole exploration mission, transmitting invaluable data and images.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent Supreme Court ruling on GST compensation, consider the following statements:
1. The ruling reinforces the principles of fiscal federalism.
2. It primarily addresses the Centre's obligations and states' rights regarding revenue shortfalls in the post-GST compensation period.
3. The judgment explicitly states that states have absolute financial autonomy without any central oversight.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The ruling indeed reinforces fiscal federalism. Statement 2 is correct: It clarifies obligations and rights post-compensation. Statement 3 is incorrect: While upholding autonomy, it does not suggest 'absolute' autonomy without any central oversight; cooperative federalism implies a balance.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about V. S. Achuthanandan is/are correct?
1. He served as the Chief Minister of Kerala for five years.
2. He was a prominent leader of the Communist Party of India (Marxist).
3. He played a significant role in land reforms in Kerala.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. V. S. Achuthanandan served as CM from 2006-2011 (five years), was a key CPI(M) leader, and was instrumental in land reforms in Kerala.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding India's National AI Strategy 2.0:
1. It focuses primarily on military applications of Artificial Intelligence.
2. Ethical considerations and data privacy are key pillars of the strategy.
3. It aims to position India as a global leader in sustainable AI development.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The strategy focuses on diverse sectors like healthcare, agriculture, education, and governance, not primarily military. Statements 2 and 3 are correct, as the strategy emphasizes ethical development, data privacy, and global leadership in sustainable AI.

prelimsMocks.question: Dr. Sulochana Gadgil, who recently passed away, was renowned for her pioneering research in which of the following fields?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Dr. Sulochana Gadgil was a celebrated meteorologist and climate scientist, widely recognized for her pioneering research on the Indian monsoon system and its variability.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a primary objective of the newly launched 'Bharat Krishi Samriddhi Yojana'?
options: (c) Providing free agricultural machinery to all farmers.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Providing free agricultural machinery to all farmers.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The BKSY focuses on boosting agri-exports, improving post-harvest infrastructure, and promoting value-added products through financial incentives and technical assistance. While it supports farmers, providing free machinery to *all* farmers is not explicitly stated as a primary objective of this specific scheme, which targets export-oriented farming.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on GST compensation for the future of fiscal federalism in India. Discuss the challenges and opportunities for Centre-State financial relations in the post-compensation era. (150 words, 10 marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein. GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the SC ruling and its context (GST compensation). Discuss how it reinforces cooperative fiscal federalism. Analyze implications: clarity on states' rights, potential impact on 16th Finance Commission, challenges in revenue generation for states, opportunities for fiscal discipline and innovation. Conclude on balancing autonomy and national economic goals.

mainsMocks.question: Critically evaluate India's National AI Strategy 2.0. How does it aim to position India as a global leader in Artificial Intelligence, particularly with its emphasis on ethical development and inclusive growth? (250 words, 15 marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life. Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology. Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, Robotics, Nano-technology, Bio-technology and issues relating to Intellectual Property Rights.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by outlining the key features of National AI Strategy 2.0 (ethical AI, data privacy, inclusive access, R&D, workforce development). Discuss how these pillars aim to achieve global leadership (e.g., trust in Indian AI, 'AI for India' solutions). Critically evaluate potential challenges (skill gap, infrastructure, regulatory enforcement, global competition). Conclude on the strategy's potential to leverage India's digital public infrastructure and human capital.

mainsMocks.question: The passing of V. S. Achuthanandan marks the end of an era in Kerala's political landscape. Discuss his contributions to shaping the state's socio-political narrative, with a particular focus on land reforms and social justice. (250 words, 15 marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS1: Post-independence consolidation and reorganization within the country. GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure. Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce V. S. Achuthanandan as a veteran leader. Detail his long political career and association with CPI(M). Emphasize his role in Kerala's land reforms (e.g., Kerala Land Reforms Act) and its impact on social equity. Discuss his advocacy for the marginalized, anti-corruption stance, and contribution to the 'Kerala Model' of development. Conclude by summarizing his enduring legacy on Kerala's unique socio-political fabric.

quickBytes.text: The Goods and Services Tax (GST) was implemented in India on July 1, 2017.
Kerala's literacy rate is the highest among Indian states, often attributed to robust social sector investments.
The European Union (EU) is a political and economic union of 27 member states located primarily in Europe.
ISRO's Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan) was India's first interplanetary mission, launched in 2013.
The Coastal Regulation Zone (CRZ) Notification regulates activities along India's coastline to protect marine and coastal ecosystems.
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.
The Indian monsoon is a seasonal wind system that accounts for about 70% of India's annual rainfall.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national agency for incident response in cybersecurity.
The Sangeet Natak Akademi is India's national academy for music, dance, and drama.
Jal Jeevan Mission aims to provide Functional Household Tap Connections (FHTC) to every rural household by 2024.

GS1.headline: Veteran Communist Leader V. S. Achuthanandan Passes Away at 101
GS1.explainer:
• V. S. Achuthanandan, a towering figure in Kerala politics and former Chief Minister, passed away at the age of 101.
• Known for his unwavering commitment to socialist ideals and as a fierce advocate for the marginalized, he served as Chief Minister of Kerala from 2006 to 2011.
• Achuthanandan was a prominent leader of the Communist Party of India (Marxist) and a seven-time Member of the Legislative Assembly (MLA) from various constituencies in Kerala.
• His political career spanned over eight decades, making him one of India's longest-serving politicians.
• He was celebrated for his anti-corruption stances and his role in various social movements and land reforms in Kerala.
gsSubTopics: Post-Independence Consolidation and Reorganization within India
Political Philosophies
Important Personalities
Kerala Politics
prelimsPoints: V. S. Achuthanandan's role as CM of Kerala
His association with CPI(M)
Significance of Kerala's land reforms
General knowledge about prominent political figures.
mainsPoints: Discuss the contributions of V. S. Achuthanandan to Kerala's political and social landscape, particularly in the context of land reforms and social justice.
Analyze the evolution of communist politics in Kerala, using Achuthanandan's career as a case study.
Evaluate the impact of long-serving political figures on state governance and policy-making in India.
Examine the 'Kerala Model' of development and the role played by leaders like Achuthanandan in its shaping.
GS1.imageDescription: A black and white photograph of V. S. Achuthanandan from his younger days, alongside a more recent portrait.
GS1.trivia: V. S. Achuthanandan was the last surviving member of the 32 communist leaders who walked out of the CPI National Council in 1964 to form the CPI(M).

GS1.headline: Noted Meteorologist and Climate Scientist Sulochana Gadgil Dies at 81
GS1.explainer:
• Dr. Sulochana Gadgil, an internationally renowned meteorologist and climate scientist, passed away at the age of 81.
• She was widely recognized for her pioneering research on the Indian monsoon system, its variability, and its impact on agriculture.
• Dr. Gadgil's work significantly contributed to understanding monsoon dynamics, aiding in better climate prediction models.
• She was a recipient of numerous accolades, including the Padma Bhushan, for her profound contributions to atmospheric science.
• Her legacy includes mentoring generations of scientists and advocating for interdisciplinary research in climate science.
gsSubTopics: Important Personalities
Geographical phenomena
Climate Change
Indian Meteorology
prelimsPoints: Contributions of Sulochana Gadgil to Indian meteorology
Key aspects of the Indian Monsoon (e.g., factors influencing it)
Significance of monsoon for Indian agriculture
Organizations involved in meteorological research in India (e.g., IMD, IITM).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Dr. Sulochana Gadgil's research on the Indian monsoon and its impact on agriculture and climate science.
Analyze the challenges and complexities of monsoon prediction in India and how scientific advancements have improved forecasting.
Evaluate the broader implications of climate variability on India's economy and society, particularly for the agricultural sector.
Examine the role of eminent scientists in shaping national research priorities and fostering scientific temper.
GS1.imageDescription: A portrait of Dr. Sulochana Gadgil, with a subtle background image of monsoon clouds over India.
GS1.trivia: Sulochana Gadgil was the first Indian woman to be elected as a Fellow of the American Meteorological Society.

GS1.headline: Ratan Thiyam, Iconic Playwright and Theatre Director, Passes Away at 77
GS1.explainer:
• Ratan Thiyam, the legendary Indian playwright and theatre director, passed away at the age of 77, leaving an indelible mark on contemporary Indian theatre.
• Hailing from Manipur, Thiyam was celebrated for his unique theatrical style that blended traditional Manipuri forms with modern global aesthetics.
• His works often explored themes of conflict, human suffering, and cultural identity, resonating deeply with audiences worldwide.
• He was the founder-director of the Chorus Repertory Theatre and a recipient of the Padma Shri and Sangeet Natak Akademi Award.
• Thiyam's innovative use of music, movement, and visual artistry transformed Indian theatre and brought it international acclaim.
gsSubTopics: Indian Culture - Salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Important Personalities
prelimsPoints: Contributions of Ratan Thiyam to Indian theatre
Key characteristics of Manipuri theatre/dance forms
Major awards in Indian performing arts (e.g., Sangeet Natak Akademi Award)
Notable theatre personalities in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the unique contributions of Ratan Thiyam to contemporary Indian theatre, highlighting how he blended traditional forms with modern aesthetics.
Analyze how theatre, as an art form, can be used to address social and political issues, drawing examples from Thiyam's works.
Examine the role of regional artists and cultural institutions in promoting and preserving India's diverse artistic heritage.
Evaluate the challenges faced by performing arts in India today and suggest measures for their promotion and sustenance.
GS1.imageDescription: A black and white photograph of Ratan Thiyam, perhaps during a theatre rehearsal, reflecting his intense artistic vision.
GS1.trivia: Ratan Thiyam's production of 'Chakravyuh' (The Wheel of War) is considered one of his most iconic works and has been performed globally.

GS1.headline: M. K. Muthu, Actor and Politician, Passes Away at 77
GS1.explainer:
• M. K. Muthu, the veteran Tamil actor and politician, passed away at the age of 77.
• Son of former Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M. Karunanidhi, Muthu had a brief but notable career in Tamil cinema during the 1970s.
• He also ventured into politics, following in his father's footsteps, though his political journey was less prominent.
• His passing marks the end of an era for a family deeply entrenched in Tamil Nadu's political and cultural landscape.
• Muthu was known for his melodious voice and acting prowess in films like 'Pillaiyo Pillai' and 'Namma Kuzhandaigal'.
gsSubTopics: Indian Culture - Salient aspects of Art Forms
Important Personalities
Regional Politics
prelimsPoints: Brief facts about M.K. Muthu's career
Association with M. Karunanidhi
General knowledge about prominent figures in regional cinema and politics.
GS1.imageDescription: A vintage photograph of M. K. Muthu from his acting days.
GS1.trivia: M. K. Muthu was the first son of M. Karunanidhi, a five-time Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu.

GS1.headline: Chandra Barot, Director of Iconic Film 'Don', Passes Away at 86
GS1.explainer:
• Chandra Barot, the acclaimed film director best known for the 1978 Bollywood classic 'Don', passed away at the age of 86.
• His directorial debut 'Don', starring Amitabh Bachchan, became a blockbuster and cult classic, influencing generations of filmmakers.
• Barot was also known for his work as an assistant director to renowned filmmakers like Manoj Kumar.
• His demise marks the loss of a significant voice from the golden era of Hindi cinema.
• The original 'Don' was celebrated for its gripping screenplay, memorable dialogues, and iconic music.
gsSubTopics: Indian Culture - Salient aspects of Art Forms
Important Personalities
Film Industry
prelimsPoints: Chandra Barot's most famous film 'Don'
Key figures in Hindi cinema history
General knowledge about iconic Indian films.
GS1.imageDescription: A still from the movie 'Don' featuring Amitabh Bachchan, with Chandra Barot's name highlighted.
GS1.trivia: The 1978 film 'Don' was remade in 2006 with Shah Rukh Khan in the lead, also becoming a commercial success.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Federal Fiscal Autonomy in Landmark Ruling on GST Compensation
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today delivered a pivotal judgment, reinforcing the principles of fiscal federalism by ruling on the modalities of Goods and Services Tax (GST) compensation to states.
• The ruling clarifies the Centre's obligations and states' rights regarding revenue shortfalls, particularly in the post-GST compensation period.
• It emphasizes cooperative federalism while safeguarding the financial independence of state governments, drawing attention to Article 280 and the Finance Commission's role.
• Legal experts suggest the judgment will have long-term implications for Centre-State financial relations and future tax reforms, potentially influencing the 16th Finance Commission's recommendations.
• The verdict comes amidst ongoing debates about the distribution of financial resources and states' fiscal space, providing much-needed clarity.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution - Federalism
Centre-State Financial Relations
GST Council
Fiscal Policy
Judicial Review
Finance Commission
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court's role in federal disputes
Concepts of GST compensation and its constitutional basis
Constitutional provisions related to Centre-State financial relations (e.g., Articles 268-281)
Role and mandate of the Finance Commission
Meaning of fiscal autonomy and its importance in federal structure.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's judgment on fiscal federalism in India, discussing its impact on the distribution of financial resources.
Discuss how the ruling balances the Centre's financial obligations with states' fiscal autonomy, particularly in the context of revenue sharing and tax devolution.
Evaluate the potential impact on future GST Council deliberations and inter-state economic disparities, considering the shift in compensation mechanisms.
Examine the role of the judiciary in resolving Centre-State disputes and upholding constitutional principles related to federal structure and financial governance.
GS2.imageDescription: An illustration depicting the Supreme Court of India alongside a map of India highlighting Centre-State financial flows and the GST logo.
GS2.trivia: The concept of GST compensation was initially for five years, ending in June 2022, but states have continuously sought extensions or alternative mechanisms due to revenue uncertainties.

GS2.headline: India-EU FTA Negotiations Progress Towards Key Chapters
GS2.explainer:
• Talks for the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) showed significant progress today, with negotiators reportedly nearing consensus on crucial chapters.
• Key areas of discussion included market access for goods, services, investment protection, and intellectual property rights.
• Both sides expressed optimism about bridging remaining gaps, aiming for a comprehensive and mutually beneficial agreement.
• An FTA with the EU, India's third-largest trading partner, is expected to boost bilateral trade and investment significantly.
• The agreement is seen as a strategic move to diversify supply chains and strengthen economic ties amidst global geopolitical shifts.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, Regional and Global Groupings and Agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of Policies and Politics of Developed and Developing Countries on India’s interests
Indian Economy and issues relating to Planning, Mobilization of Resources, Growth, Development and Employment
prelimsPoints: Current status of India-EU relations
Key objectives of FTA negotiations
Major trading partners of India
Concept of trade blocs and economic integration.
Difference between FTA, CEPA, and Customs Union.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of a comprehensive India-EU Free Trade Agreement for India's foreign policy and economic growth.
Discuss the potential challenges and opportunities for various sectors of the Indian economy arising from the FTA.
Evaluate how this agreement aligns with India's broader trade policy objectives and its role in global supply chain diversification.
Examine the role of FTAs in shaping India's international relations and its position in the multilateral trading system.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union superimposed over a graph showing upward trade trends.
GS2.trivia: Negotiations for an India-EU FTA were initially launched in 2007 but stalled in 2013 before being officially re-launched in 2022.

GS2.headline: New Guidelines for Online Gaming Industry to Ensure Consumer Protection and Responsible Gaming
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) today released new comprehensive guidelines for the online gaming industry in India.
• The guidelines aim to ensure consumer protection, prevent addiction, and promote responsible gaming practices, particularly for minors.
• Key provisions include mandatory KYC verification, self-regulatory mechanisms, and transparent disclosure of game odds and financial risks.
• The framework also addresses issues of data privacy, grievance redressal, and the prohibition of games involving betting or gambling where prohibited by law.
• This move is expected to bring greater regulatory clarity and foster a safe and sustainable growth environment for the burgeoning online gaming sector.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Welfare Schemes for Vulnerable Sections of the population by the Centre and States and the Performance of these Schemes
Role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Cyber Security
GS2.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: Role of MeitY in regulating digital services
Importance of consumer protection in digital economy
Concept of KYC verification
Challenges of online gaming (e.g., addiction, financial fraud)
Regulatory framework for emerging digital sectors.
mainsPoints: Discuss the need for new guidelines for the online gaming industry in India, considering its rapid growth and associated social and economic challenges.
Analyze the key provisions of the new guidelines and evaluate their potential effectiveness in ensuring consumer protection and promoting responsible gaming.
Examine the role of self-regulatory bodies and technology in fostering a safe and ethical online gaming environment, particularly for vulnerable populations.
Critically assess the balance between regulating the online gaming sector and allowing for innovation and economic growth within the digital economy.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized graphic showing a mobile phone screen displaying a game, with a shield icon overlayed, representing protection.
GS2.trivia: India's online gaming market is one of the fastest-growing globally, projected to reach multi-billion-dollar valuations in the coming years.

GS2.headline: Report Highlights Progress in Jal Jeevan Mission, Focus on Quality Standards
GS2.explainer:
• A recent report released by the Ministry of Jal Shakti highlights significant progress in the implementation of the Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM) across rural India.
• The mission has achieved a major milestone in providing tap water connections to a large percentage of rural households since its inception.
• The report specifically emphasizes the enhanced focus on ensuring the quality of water supplied, with increased testing and community participation in monitoring.
• Challenges related to source sustainability and maintenance of infrastructure in remote areas were also acknowledged, with plans for further interventions.
• JJM aims to provide safe and adequate drinking water through individual household tap connections by 2024, a key social welfare initiative.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Welfare Schemes for Vulnerable Sections of the population by the Centre and States and the Performance of these Schemes
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Water Management
prelimsPoints: Objectives and targets of Jal Jeevan Mission
Ministry responsible for JJM
Concept of 'Har Ghar Jal'
Importance of water quality testing
Challenges in rural water supply.
mainsPoints: Discuss the progress and impact of the Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM) in transforming rural water supply in India, highlighting its achievements and challenges.
Analyze the importance of focusing on water quality standards and source sustainability for the long-term success of such a large-scale welfare scheme.
Evaluate the role of community participation and local governance in the effective implementation and maintenance of rural water infrastructure.
Examine how access to safe and adequate drinking water contributes to public health, women's empowerment, and overall rural development.
GS2.imageDescription: A visual showing a rural Indian woman happily filling water from a tap connection, symbolizing improved access.
GS2.trivia: Jal Jeevan Mission was launched in August 2019 with the vision to provide safe and adequate drinking water to every rural household in India by 2024.

GS2.headline: India and Japan Strengthen Strategic Partnership with New Defence Cooperation Initiatives
GS2.explainer:
• India and Japan today announced new initiatives to bolster their strategic partnership, primarily focusing on enhanced defence cooperation and security collaboration.
• The discussions, held at a high-level bilateral meeting, included joint military exercises, technology transfer, and greater interoperability between their armed forces.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, emphasizing maritime security and regional stability.
• The enhanced partnership also explores cooperation in emerging technologies and critical infrastructure protection.
• This move signifies a deepening alignment between two key regional powers, aiming to counter regional security challenges and promote a rules-based international order.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, Regional and Global Groupings and Agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of Policies and Politics of Developed and Developing Countries on India’s interests
Security challenges and their management in border areas
Defence Technology
prelimsPoints: Key aspects of India-Japan strategic partnership
Importance of Indo-Pacific region
Major joint military exercises between India and Japan
Concept of a 'rules-based international order'
Significance of defense technology cooperation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the growing strategic convergence between India and Japan, particularly in the context of defence cooperation and their shared vision for the Indo-Pacific.
Discuss the implications of enhanced India-Japan security ties for regional stability and the balance of power in Asia.
Evaluate the potential for technology transfer and joint development in defence between the two nations, considering India's 'Make in India' initiative.
Examine how this bilateral partnership contributes to multilateral initiatives like the Quad and reinforces a rules-based international order.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan side-by-side, with a naval vessel in the background, symbolizing maritime and defense cooperation.
GS2.trivia: India and Japan are both members of the Quadrilateral Security Dialogue (Quad), along with the United States and Australia.

GS2.headline: Dr. P. Namperumalsamy, Visionary Ophthalmologist, Passes Away at 85
GS2.explainer:
• Dr. P. Namperumalsamy, a globally acclaimed ophthalmologist and pioneer in community eye care, passed away at 85.
• He was the founder of Aravind Eye Care System, which revolutionized eye care delivery in India and globally through its high-volume, high-quality, and affordable model.
• His 'camps-based' approach significantly reduced preventable blindness, especially among the rural poor.
• Dr. Namperumalsamy was a recipient of the Padma Bhushan and the Public Service Award from the American Academy of Ophthalmology.
• His work transformed the landscape of public health, making quality eye care accessible to millions.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Welfare Schemes for Vulnerable Sections
Important Personalities
prelimsPoints: Contributions of Dr. P. Namperumalsamy to ophthalmology and public health
Aravind Eye Care System model
Concept of 'preventable blindness'
Government initiatives for eye care in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the innovative model of the Aravind Eye Care System as pioneered by Dr. P. Namperumalsamy and its impact on public health in India.
Analyze how a high-volume, high-quality, and affordable healthcare delivery system can address the challenges of accessibility and equity in developing countries.
Evaluate the role of social entrepreneurship in solving critical public health issues like preventable blindness.
Examine the lessons that can be drawn from the Aravind model for other healthcare sectors in India.
GS2.imageDescription: A photograph of Dr. P. Namperumalsamy, smiling, with a subtle background of people receiving eye care.
GS2.trivia: Aravind Eye Care System performs over 5 million outpatient visits and 500,000 surgeries annually, making it one of the largest eye care providers globally.

GS2.headline: C. V. Padmarajan, Former Kerala Minister and Veteran Politician, Passes Away at 93
GS2.explainer:
• C. V. Padmarajan, a seasoned politician and former minister in Kerala, passed away at the age of 93.
• He was a prominent leader of the Indian National Congress in Kerala, known for his administrative acumen and parliamentary skills.
• Padmarajan held various ministerial portfolios during his career, contributing to several key policy decisions in the state.
• His political journey spanned several decades, witnessing significant transformations in Kerala's political landscape.
• He was respected across party lines for his integrity and dedication to public service.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution - Historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Parliament and State Legislatures - structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Important Personalities

General knowledge about former state ministers
Role of state legislatures.
GS2.imageDescription: A formal portrait of C. V. Padmarajan.
GS2.trivia: Kerala has a history of coalition governments, with both the Congress-led UDF and CPI(M)-led LDF alternating in power.
